name.ja: ゲーデルの二分律 (1951 Gibbs Lecture)

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: Horn A (mind develops) と Horn B (absolute undecidable) が inclusive-or で共存可能。Rei の axisT lifecycle で statement-distributive に分散される
reiTyping.peaceCheckReason: 純粋メタ哲学命題、応用なし

falsifiability: もし数学的活動 (formal proof generation) が機械的に有限規則で完全捕捉できると示されれば Horn A 反証。もしすべての Diophantine 問題が無矛盾拡張で解決可能と示されれば Horn B 反証
culturalNote: Gödel 1951 Gibbs Lecture (Brown University). Collected Works Vol. III に収録 (Oxford UP, 1995). Lucas-Penrose 議論 / Feferman / Koellner 注釈で発展継続

description.ja: Gödel が 1951 Gibbs Lecture で提示した二分律: 数学は完結不可能 (Horn A) であるか、絶対的に決定不能な Diophantine 問題が存在する (Horn B) か。Gödel 自身は Horn A 信奉。Lucas/Penrose 論争 (mind ≠ machine) の起点。Rei-AIOS は axisT lifecycle で両 horn の statement-distributive 共存を提案 (Lifecycle Disjunction Projection 仮説)
description.en: Disjunction presented by Gödel in 1951 Gibbs Lecture: Either mathematics is incompletable (Horn A) or there exist absolutely undecidable Diophantine problems (Horn B). Gödel favored Horn A. Origin of Lucas-Penrose debate (mind ≠ machine). Rei-AIOS proposes via axisT lifecycle a statement-distributive coexistence of both horns (Lifecycle Disjunction Projection hypothesis)

ramanujanFormula.interpretation: ゲーデルの二分律は、否定の否定を通じて、両つの角が時間軸上で共存する論理構造を示す。

visualGlyph.svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 100 100' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><defs><polygon points='0 0, 10 3, 0 6' fill='#00d4ff'/></defs><circle cx='30' cy='50' r='12' fill='none' stroke='#7c3aed' stroke-width='2'/><circle cx='70' cy='50' r='12' fill='none' stroke='#00ff88' stroke-width='2'/><text x='27' y='54' font-size='10' fill='#7c3aed' font-weight='bold'>A</text><text x='67' y='54' font-size='10' fill='#00ff88' font-weight='bold'>B</text><line x1='42' y1='50' x2='58' y2='50' stroke='#ec4899' stroke-width='2.5' marker-end='url(#arrowhead)'/><circle cx='50' cy='50' r='20' fill='none' stroke='#ffd700' stroke-width='1.5' stroke-dasharray='3,2'/><path d='M 50 70 Q 45 80 50 90' stroke='#00d4ff' stroke-width='1.5' fill='none' stroke-linecap='round'/><path d='M 50 70 Q 55 80 50 90' stroke='#00ff88' stroke-width='1.5' fill='none' stroke-linecap='round'/><text x='48' y='98' font-size='7' fill='#f59e0b' text-anchor='middle'>axisT</text></svg>
visualGlyph.interpretation: 左は Horn A（心の成長可能性）、右は Horn B（絶対決定不能）。中心の共鳴場で両者が時間軸に沿って分散共存する。

counterPositionsDetail.argumentSummary: mind ≠ Turing machine; consciousness transcends formal systems
neither horn is forced; the disjunction admits a third path via reflective extensions
modal-logic reconstruction; epistemic vs ontic horn-A distinction
